articles.headline: Tensions Flare Along LoC: India, Pakistan Exchange Fire in Border Skirmish
articles.explainer:
• Indian and Pakistani soldiers briefly exchanged fire along the Line of Control (LoC) in the Poonch sector, marking a fresh escalation in border tensions.
• The incident involved small arms fire and light shelling, with no immediate reports of casualties on either side.
• Both nations have accused each other of initiating the firing, leading to diplomatic protests.
• The skirmish comes amidst ongoing efforts to maintain a fragile ceasefire agreement, raising concerns about regional stability.
• Security analysts suggest the incident might be a localized provocation or a response to perceived cross-border activities.
gsSubTopics: Border areas
India and its neighbourhood- relations
Security challenges and their management in border areas
prelimsPoints: Line of Control (LoC) is a de facto border between India and Pakistan in Jammu and Kashmir.
The 2003 ceasefire agreement between India and Pakistan aimed to reduce cross-border firing.
Poonch sector is a frequently active area along the LoC.
Indian Army and Pakistan Army are the primary forces deployed along the LoC.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of LoC skirmishes on India-Pakistan bilateral relations and regional stability.
Discuss the challenges in maintaining peace along the LoC and the effectiveness of existing confidence-building measures.
Examine India's border management strategy and the role of diplomatic channels in de-escalation.
Evaluate the internal security ramifications of increased border tensions for Jammu and Kashmir.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Army soldiers patrolling a forward post along the Line of Control in a mountainous region.
articles.trivia: The Line of Control (LoC) was established after the Simla Agreement of 1972, replacing the earlier ceasefire line.

articles.headline: Veteran Space Scientist Dr. K. Kasturirangan Passes Away at 84
articles.explainer:
• Dr. Krishnaswamy Kasturirangan, a towering figure in Indian space science and former Chairman of ISRO, passed away at the age of 84.
• He spearheaded India's space program during a critical phase, including the development of the PSLV and initial work on Chandrayaan-1.
• Dr. Kasturirangan also played a crucial role in shaping India's education policy, chairing the committee that drafted the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• His contributions extended to various scientific and governmental advisory roles, earning him numerous national and international accolades.
• The scientific community and national leadership mourned the loss of a visionary leader and institution builder.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Space technology
prelimsPoints: Dr. K. Kasturirangan was Chairman of ISRO from 1994 to 2003.
He oversaw the successful launch of the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV) and India's first moon mission, Chandrayaan-1.
He chaired the committee that drafted the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Awarded Padma Vibhushan, India's second-highest civilian honor.
mainsPoints: Discuss the impact of Dr. K. Kasturirangan's leadership on the growth and achievements of India's space program.
Analyze the intersection of science and policy, citing his contributions to both space technology and education reform.
Evaluate the role of visionary scientists in nation-building and fostering scientific temper in India.
Examine the legacy of India's space pioneers in achieving self-reliance in critical technologies.
articles.imageDescription: A portrait of Dr. K. Kasturirangan, a distinguished scientist, smiling.
articles.trivia: Dr. Kasturirangan's tenure as ISRO Chairman saw India launch its first operational remote sensing satellites and communication satellites using indigenous launchers.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science & Technology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Indian Space Programme and its pioneers

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Environmental Clearance for Critical Coastal Zone Project
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today upheld the environmental clearance granted to a major infrastructure project in a Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) area, dismissing petitions challenging its ecological impact.
• The ruling emphasized the need for a balanced approach between environmental protection and developmental imperatives, citing the project's strategic importance.
• The Court noted that all due processes under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, and CRZ Notifications were followed.
• Environmental activists expressed concern over the precedent set, while the government welcomed the decision as a boost for infrastructure development.
• The judgment highlighted the role of expert committees in assessing environmental impact and the judiciary's limited scope in technical matters.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
Environmental protection acts
prelimsPoints: CRZ notifications regulate activities in coastal areas to protect the marine environment.
The Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, is an umbrella legislation for environmental protection.
Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) is mandatory for certain projects to obtain environmental clearance.
National Green Tribunal (NGT) deals with environmental cases.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in balancing economic development and environmental conservation in India, with reference to the CRZ framework.
Analyze the role of the judiciary in environmental governance and the implications of judicial pronouncements on developmental projects.
Critically evaluate the effectiveness of the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process in ensuring sustainable development.
Examine the 'development vs. environment' debate in India and the need for robust regulatory mechanisms.
articles.imageDescription: A coastal line showing a mix of natural environment and an ongoing infrastructure project.
articles.trivia: The first Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) notification was issued in 1991 under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Signals Cautious Stance Amid Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
• RBI Governor cited persistent global uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility, as key reasons for the cautious approach.
• The central bank also maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, indicating a continued focus on bringing inflation within target.
• Analysts anticipate that this stability will support economic growth while keeping inflationary pressures in check.
• The decision reflects a measured strategy to navigate complex domestic and international economic landscapes.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: The repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
The primary objective of the RBI's monetary policy is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target set by the government for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for inflation and economic growth.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the need to support economic recovery amidst global headwinds.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflationary pressures and demand-side imbalances.
Examine the coordination between fiscal and monetary policies in achieving macroeconomic stability in India.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) logo with a graph showing stable interest rates.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Functions of RBI and Monetary Policy Committee

articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Shows Promising Progress in Pilot Projects
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced significant progress in the pilot projects under the National Green Hydrogen Mission, launched earlier this year.
• Several industrial clusters and research institutions have successfully demonstrated green hydrogen production and application in various sectors like refining and fertilizer.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Government incentives and public-private partnerships are driving innovation and investment in this nascent but crucial sector.
• Challenges remain in scaling up production, reducing costs, and developing a robust supply chain, but initial results are encouraging.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Energy security
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced through electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for India to become a global hub for green hydrogen.
It is considered a key component for achieving India's net-zero emissions target by 2070.
Applications include fuel for transportation, industrial feedstock, and power generation.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the potential of green hydrogen as a game-changer for India's energy security and climate change mitigation efforts.
Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission in achieving India's ambitious decarbonization targets.
Analyze the economic and technological challenges associated with scaling up green hydrogen production and its integration into the existing energy infrastructure.
Examine the role of government policies and international collaborations in fostering the growth of India's green hydrogen economy.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but it rarely exists in its pure form on Earth.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) for Future Missions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful test flight of its advanced Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) demonstrator.
• This critical test focused on validating autonomous landing capabilities and re-entry technologies, crucial for cost-effective space access.
• The RLV program aims to develop fully reusable orbital launch vehicles, significantly reducing the cost of satellite launches.
• This achievement marks a major step towards India's long-term goal of developing sustainable and competitive space transportation systems.
• Future phases will involve orbital flights and integration of propulsion systems for operational use.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Space technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) is a winged body aerospace vehicle.
The objective is to achieve low-cost access to space.
RLV technology is crucial for future human spaceflight missions like Gaganyaan.
It aims to make space missions more frequent and economical by reusing vehicle components.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's future space ambitions and its potential impact on the global space market.
Analyze how RLV development aligns with India's strategic goals of achieving self-reliance and commercial competitiveness in space technology.
Examine the technological challenges and breakthroughs required for developing fully reusable launch systems.
Evaluate the economic benefits and environmental advantages of reusable space launch vehicles compared to expendable ones.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) demonstrator landing on a runway.
articles.trivia: The first RLV-TD mission, HEX (Hypersonic Flight Experiment), was successfully conducted by ISRO in 2016.

articles.headline: India-Japan Summit Focuses on Indo-Pacific Cooperation and Economic Ties
articles.explainer:
• Prime Minister of India and Japan's Premier held a bilateral summit in New Delhi, reaffirming their commitment to a Free and Open Indo-Pacific.
• Key discussions revolved around enhancing security cooperation, maritime domain awareness, and strengthening supply chain resilience in the region.
• Both leaders emphasized deepening economic partnership, particularly in areas of clean energy, digital technology, and infrastructure development.
• Agreements were signed to boost collaboration in defense manufacturing and facilitate Japanese investment in India's North-Eastern states.
• The summit underscored the growing strategic alignment between India and Japan in addressing regional and global challenges.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan share a 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership'.
Both countries are members of the Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) along with the USA and Australia.
Japan is a major investor in India's infrastructure projects, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.
The Indo-Pacific region is crucial for global trade and security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic convergence between India and Japan in the context of the Indo-Pacific region and its implications for regional security.
Discuss the multifaceted nature of India-Japan bilateral relations, covering economic, security, and cultural dimensions.
Evaluate the role of India and Japan in promoting a rules-based international order and countering challenges to regional stability.
Examine the potential for enhanced cooperation between India and Japan in critical and emerging technologies and defense manufacturing.
articles.imageDescription: Prime Ministers of India and Japan shaking hands at a joint press conference.
articles.trivia: Japan is one of the few countries with which India holds a '2+2' Ministerial Dialogue (Foreign and Defence Ministers).

articles.headline: Union Health Ministry Rolls Out New Policy to Tackle Non-Communicable Diseases
articles.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry launched a comprehensive new national policy aimed at strengthening prevention, diagnosis, and management of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs).
• The policy focuses on a multi-sectoral approach, integrating health promotion, early screening, and improved access to treatment for NCDs like diabetes, hypertension, and cancer.
• Key initiatives include expanding wellness centers under Ayushman Bharat and leveraging digital health platforms for patient management and data collection.
• Special emphasis is placed on lifestyle modifications, tobacco and alcohol control, and reducing unhealthy dietary practices.
• The move is crucial as NCDs account for a significant portion of morbidity and mortality in India, posing a major public health challenge.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
keyTerms: Ayushman Bharat Health and Wellness Centres
prelimsPoints: NCDs include cardiovascular diseases, cancers, chronic respiratory diseases, and diabetes.
Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY) provides health insurance cover.
Health and Wellness Centres (HWCs) under Ayushman Bharat aim to provide comprehensive primary healthcare.
India faces a dual burden of communicable and non-communicable diseases.
mainsPoints: Analyze the growing burden of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in India and its socio-economic implications.
Critically evaluate the key provisions of the new NCD policy and its potential effectiveness in addressing the challenge.
Discuss the role of public health infrastructure, digital health, and community participation in successful NCD prevention and management.
Examine how lifestyle factors and socio-economic determinants contribute to the prevalence of NCDs and what multi-sectoral interventions are needed.
articles.imageDescription: A medical professional conducting a health check-up, symbolizing NCD prevention.
articles.trivia: Globally, NCDs kill 41 million people each year, equivalent to 74% of all deaths.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Issues
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government policies and interventions for health sector

articles.headline: Government Extends PM-KISAN Scheme Benefits to More Marginal Farmers
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved an expansion of the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) scheme, bringing more marginal and small farmers under its ambit.
• The revised guidelines aim to address existing inclusion-exclusion errors and ensure equitable distribution of financial assistance.
• PM-KISAN provides income support to farmer families to supplement their financial needs for agricultural inputs and domestic expenses.
• This move is expected to further boost rural incomes and alleviate financial distress among vulnerable farming communities.
• The government also announced measures to streamline the verification process and ensure timely disbursement of funds.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: PM-KISAN is a central sector scheme providing income support to all landholding farmer families.
Eligible farmer families receive ₹6,000 per year in three equal installments of ₹2,000.
The scheme is implemented through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) mode.
Exclusion criteria exist for certain categories of farmers, like institutional landholders and high-income taxpayers.
mainsPoints: Analyze the impact of income support schemes like PM-KISAN on farmer welfare and agricultural productivity in India.
Discuss the challenges in identifying eligible beneficiaries and ensuring effective implementation of direct benefit transfer schemes in agriculture.
Evaluate the role of government subsidies in addressing agrarian distress and promoting sustainable agricultural practices.
Examine the broader implications of such schemes for rural development and poverty reduction in India.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field with a digital payment symbol overlaid, representing PM-KISAN.
articles.trivia: PM-KISAN was launched on 24 February 2019, with retrospective effect from 1 December 2018.

articles.headline: Ancient Buddhist Stupa Unearthed in Bihar, Sheds Light on Mauryan Era
articles.explainer:
• Archaeologists have discovered a well-preserved ancient Buddhist stupa in the Vaishali district of Bihar, dating back to the Mauryan period.
• The excavation revealed intricate carvings, pottery fragments, and possibly relics, providing new insights into early Buddhist architecture and practices.
• This discovery is significant for understanding the spread of Buddhism during the reign of Emperor Ashoka and the cultural landscape of ancient Magadha.
• The site is expected to become a major archaeological and pilgrimage destination, boosting local tourism and research opportunities.
• Conservation efforts are underway to protect the newly found structure and surrounding artifacts.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Ancient Indian History
prelimsPoints: Stupas are dome-shaped structures used as a place of meditation or for housing relics of the Buddha or his disciples.
The Mauryan Empire (c. 322–185 BCE) was a geographically extensive Iron Age historical power in ancient India.
Emperor Ashoka is credited with promoting Buddhism and constructing numerous stupas and pillars.
Bihar is historically significant as the birthplace of Buddhism and Jainism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in re-evaluating our understanding of ancient Indian history and culture, with specific reference to Buddhist sites.
Analyze the contributions of the Mauryan Empire to Indian art, architecture, and the spread of religious philosophies.
Examine the challenges and opportunities associated with preserving and promoting archaeological heritage sites in India.
Evaluate the role of Bihar as a historical cradle of religious and cultural movements in ancient India.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists excavating an ancient stupa, revealing its dome structure and surrounding artifacts.
articles.trivia: Vaishali is considered one of the earliest republics in the world and was a prominent center of Buddhism and Jainism.

articles.headline: Digital India Initiative Achieves New Milestones in Public Service Delivery
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) reported significant achievements under the Digital India program, particularly in enhancing public service delivery.
• New data shows a substantial increase in the number of digital transactions and online access to government services across various sectors.
• Initiatives like Aadhaar-enabled services, UPI payments, and the DigiLocker platform have streamlined processes and reduced corruption.
• The program has played a crucial role in improving governance, financial inclusion, and bridging the digital divide, especially in rural areas.
• Challenges related to digital literacy, cybersecurity, and infrastructure in remote regions continue to be addressed through ongoing policy interventions.
gsSubTopics: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: Digital India is a flagship program of the Government of India launched in 2015.
Its vision is to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.
Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identity number issued by UIDAI.
UPI is an instant real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative impact of the Digital India initiative on governance, public service delivery, and citizen engagement in India.
Analyze the successes and challenges faced by the Digital India program in bridging the digital divide and ensuring equitable access to digital services.
Evaluate the role of key digital platforms like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker in promoting financial inclusion and transparency.
Examine the future prospects and potential areas of improvement for India's digital governance ecosystem, considering cybersecurity and data privacy concerns.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting various digital services connecting citizens to government offices.
articles.trivia: The 'JAM Trinity' (Jan Dhan-Aadhaar-Mobile) is a key enabler of the Digital India vision, facilitating direct benefit transfers.

articles.headline: Smart Cities Mission Project Inaugurated in Pune, Focus on Sustainable Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• A significant project under the Smart Cities Mission was inaugurated in Pune, focusing on integrated command and control centers and sustainable urban mobility solutions.
• The initiative aims to enhance the city's liveability, promote efficient resource management, and improve public safety through technology.
• This project is part of Pune's larger Smart City plan, which emphasizes citizen participation and innovative urban governance models.
• Funding for the project comes from both central and state governments, along with private sector contributions.
• The successful implementation is expected to serve as a model for other cities participating in the Smart Cities Mission.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Development processes and the development industry- the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
prelimsPoints: Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015 to promote cities that provide core infrastructure, a clean and sustainable environment, and a decent quality of life.
The mission aims to drive economic growth and improve the quality of life of people by enabling local area development and harnessing technology.
Integrated Command and Control Centres (ICCCs) are a key component for smart city operations.
Pune is one of the 100 cities selected under the Smart Cities Mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and achievements of the Smart Cities Mission in India, citing examples of successful projects.
Analyze the challenges faced by urban local bodies in implementing Smart City projects, including funding, governance, and citizen engagement.
Evaluate the role of technology and innovation in transforming urban spaces and addressing issues like traffic congestion, waste management, and public safety.
Examine how the Smart Cities Mission contributes to India's broader sustainable development goals and urban planning strategies.
articles.imageDescription: A modern cityscape of Pune with smart traffic signals and green spaces, symbolizing urban development.
articles.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission covers 100 cities across India, selected through a competitive 'City Challenge' process.

articles.headline: India's Indigenous Defence Production Boosted with New Naval Destroyer Launch
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Navy officially launched its latest indigenously-built guided-missile destroyer, 'INS Vajra', at Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd (MDL).
• This launch marks a significant milestone in India's 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives for the defense sector.
• The destroyer is equipped with advanced stealth features, state-of-the-art weapon systems, and sensors, enhancing India's naval capabilities.
• The project has generated substantial employment and fostered technological self-reliance in warship building.
• 'INS Vajra' will undergo further sea trials before its formal commissioning into the fleet, bolstering India's maritime security.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: Project 15B is a class of stealth guided-missile destroyers being built for the Indian Navy.
Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd (MDL) is a premier shipyard in India.
'Make in India' in defence aims to reduce import dependence and boost domestic manufacturing.
Indian Navy is a crucial component of India's maritime security strategy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous defence production for India's strategic autonomy and national security, with special reference to naval shipbuilding.
Analyze the impact of 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives on the Indian defence manufacturing sector.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in developing advanced naval platforms and integrating cutting-edge technologies.
Examine the role of the Indian Navy in projecting power, ensuring maritime security, and protecting India's economic interests in the Indo-Pacific region.
articles.imageDescription: A newly launched guided-missile destroyer, 'INS Vajra', at a shipyard, with naval officers observing.
articles.trivia: India is one of the few countries with the capability to design and build advanced warships, including aircraft carriers and destroyers.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Indigenous defence production and Indian Navy

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Progress Reviewed by Ministry
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a comprehensive review of the implementation progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across states and Union Territories.
• The review highlighted advancements in curriculum reforms, vocational education integration, and digital learning initiatives.
• Challenges persist in teacher training, infrastructure development, and ensuring equitable access to quality education, particularly in remote areas.
• The NEP 2020 aims for holistic and multidisciplinary education, focusing on critical thinking and skill development from foundational to higher education levels.
• The Ministry reiterated its commitment to accelerating implementation, with a focus on collaborative efforts between central and state governments.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
It aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
Key features include a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure, vocational education integration, and emphasis on mother tongue as medium of instruction.
The policy seeks to increase the Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education to 50% by 2035.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the progress and challenges in the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in transforming India's education system.
Discuss how NEP 2020 addresses the skill gap and promotes holistic development among students, aligning with the demands of the 21st-century economy.
Evaluate the role of digital learning and technology in achieving the objectives of NEP 2020, especially in ensuring equitable access to quality education.
Examine the collaborative framework between central and state governments required for successful, large-scale educational reforms like NEP 2020.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting engaged in learning, symbolizing education reform.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century and aims to address the many growing developmental imperatives of India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Issues
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government policies and interventions for education sector

articles.headline: UN Report Highlights Global Progress and Challenges in Achieving SDGs
articles.explainer:
• A new United Nations report assessed global progress towards the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), noting mixed results five years before the 2030 deadline.
• While significant strides have been made in areas like poverty reduction and access to education, challenges persist in climate action, gender equality, and reducing inequalities.
• The report emphasized the disproportionate impact of global crises, such as pandemics and conflicts, on vulnerable populations and SDG achievement.
• It called for accelerated efforts, increased international cooperation, and innovative financing mechanisms to meet the ambitious targets.
• India's efforts in achieving several SDGs, particularly in clean energy and digital infrastructure, were acknowledged, though areas like hunger and health need further impetus.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are a collection of 17 interlinked global goals designed to be a 'blueprint to achieve a better and more sustainable future for all'.
They were set by the United Nations General Assembly in 2015 and are intended to be achieved by the year 2030.
NITI Aayog monitors India's progress on the SDGs.
The goals cover social, economic and environmental aspects.
mainsPoints: Analyze the global progress and persistent challenges in achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by 2030, in light of recent global crises.
Discuss India's contributions and specific challenges in meeting the SDG targets, especially in areas like poverty, hunger, and climate action.
Evaluate the role of international cooperation and multilateral institutions like the UN in fostering sustainable development across nations.
Examine the interconnectedness of the 17 SDGs and how progress in one area can influence others, emphasizing the need for integrated policy approaches.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the 17 Sustainable Development Goals icons, symbolizing global collaboration.
articles.trivia: The SDGs succeeded the Millennium Development Goals (MDGs), which ran from 2000 to 2015.

articles.headline: Phase III of Namami Gange Programme Launched with Focus on Tributary Clean-up
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti launched the third phase of the Namami Gange Programme, shifting focus to the rejuvenation of the Ganga's major tributaries.
• This phase will emphasize decentralized wastewater management, afforestation along tributary banks, and community participation in river conservation.
• The previous phases primarily focused on the main stem of the Ganga, achieving significant progress in reducing pollution and developing infrastructure.
• The initiative aims to improve the ecological health of the entire Ganga basin, ensuring sustainable water resources and biodiversity.
• New technologies for real-time water quality monitoring and public awareness campaigns are integral to the expanded program.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
River interlinking
prelimsPoints: Namami Gange Programme is an integrated conservation mission approved by the Union Government in June 2014.
It aims at effective abatement of pollution, conservation, and rejuvenation of the National River Ganga.
The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) is the implementing agency.
Key tributaries of Ganga include Yamuna, Ghaghara, Gandak, Kosi, and Son.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and effectiveness of the Namami Gange Programme in achieving its objectives of Ganga rejuvenation, highlighting the shift in focus to tributaries.
Analyze the multi-sectoral approach required for river conservation, encompassing pollution control, ecological restoration, and community engagement.
Evaluate the challenges in managing the vast Ganga basin, considering industrial pollution, agricultural runoff, and urban wastewater discharge.
Examine the importance of public participation and technological interventions in ensuring the long-term sustainability of river cleaning initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: A clean section of the Ganga river with green banks, symbolizing successful rejuvenation efforts.
articles.trivia: The Ganga is India's longest river, flowing for over 2,500 kilometers from the Himalayas to the Bay of Bengal.

articles.headline: India and ASEAN Nations Discuss Deeper Trade & Investment Pact
articles.explainer:
• India engaged in high-level discussions with the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) to explore opportunities for a deeper trade and investment agreement.
• The talks aimed at reviewing the existing ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA) agreement and identifying new sectors for enhanced cooperation.
• Focus areas included digital trade, supply chain resilience, green technologies, and greater market access for goods and services.
• Both sides expressed commitment to fostering economic integration and strengthening connectivity across the Indo-Pacific region.
• A potential upgrade to the AIFTA could significantly boost bilateral trade, which has seen steady growth over the past decade.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
India and its neighbourhood- relations
prelimsPoints: ASEAN is a regional intergovernmental organization comprising ten Southeast Asian countries.
India's 'Act East Policy' focuses on extended neighbourhood in Asia-Pacific.
AIFTA (ASEAN-India Free Trade Area) agreement came into force in 2010 for goods and 2015 for services and investment.
RCEP (Regional Comprehensive Economic Partnership) is a free trade agreement between the Asia-Pacific nations, which India chose not to join.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of India's economic engagement with ASEAN nations in the context of its 'Act East Policy' and the evolving Indo-Pacific dynamics.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges of upgrading the ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA) agreement for India's trade and manufacturing sectors.
Evaluate the role of regional trade agreements in fostering economic integration and strengthening supply chain resilience in the post-pandemic era.
Examine how India's bilateral and multilateral trade policies are shaping its position in the global economic architecture.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN nations arranged in a circle, symbolizing cooperation.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was established on 8 August 1967, in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration).

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Records Robust Growth, Contributes to Job Creation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism released data indicating a strong rebound and robust growth in India's tourism sector, surpassing pre-pandemic levels.
• International tourist arrivals and domestic travel have seen significant increases, driven by government promotional campaigns and improved infrastructure.
• The sector has emerged as a major contributor to job creation, particularly in hospitality, transport, and local artisan industries.
• Initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh' and promotion of niche tourism (adventure, eco, spiritual) have attracted diverse visitors.
• Challenges include sustainable tourism practices, skill development for tourism professionals, and enhancing global marketing efforts.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Tourism is the nodal agency for the development and promotion of tourism in India.
'Dekho Apna Desh' is an initiative to encourage citizens to travel within the country.
Tourism is a major foreign exchange earner for India.
India has a diverse range of tourist attractions, including cultural, historical, natural, and spiritual sites.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted contribution of the tourism sector to India's economy, including job creation, foreign exchange earnings, and infrastructure development.
Analyze the government's strategies and initiatives aimed at promoting tourism in India and attracting both domestic and international visitors.
Evaluate the challenges in developing a sustainable and inclusive tourism sector in India, considering environmental impact and local community benefits.
Examine the potential of niche tourism segments (e.g., medical, adventure, spiritual) in diversifying India's tourism offerings and attracting specific markets.
articles.imageDescription: A vibrant collage of famous Indian tourist destinations, showcasing diversity.
articles.trivia: The World Tourism Organization (UNWTO) defines tourism as 'travel for leisure, business, and other purposes'.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Growth and development of service sectors

highlights: India and Pakistan exchange fire along LoC, raising concerns about regional stability.
Veteran space scientist and NEP 2020 architect, Dr. K. Kasturirangan, passes away at 84.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5%, signalling a cautious approach amidst global economic uncertainties.
ISRO successfully tests its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) demonstrator, a step towards cost-effective space access.
National Green Hydrogen Mission shows promising progress in pilot projects, aiming to establish India as a global hub.

prelimsMocks.question: With reference to the recent LoC skirmish between India and Pakistan, consider the following statements:
options: (a) The Line of Control (LoC) was delineated during the Tashkent Agreement of 1966.
(b) The incident occurred in the Poonch sector, a region known for its strategic importance.
(c) The primary forces deployed along the LoC are the Border Security Force (BSF) from the Indian side and Pakistan Rangers from the Pakistani side.
(d) The 2003 ceasefire agreement between India and Pakistan is no longer in effect.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) The incident occurred in the Poonch sector, a region known for its strategic importance.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The LoC was established after the Simla Agreement of 1972, replacing the earlier ceasefire line. The primary forces deployed along the LoC are the Indian Army and Pakistan Army, while BSF and Pakistan Rangers primarily guard the international border. The 2003 ceasefire agreement has generally been in effect, though violations occur, and both sides reiterated commitment to it in 2021.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements about Dr. K. Kasturirangan's contributions is/are correct?
options: 1. He served as the Chairman of ISRO during the development of the GSLV program.
2. He chaired the committee that drafted the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
3. He was instrumental in the launch of India's first moon mission, Chandrayaan-1.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
(a) 1 and 2 only
(b) 2 and 3 only
(c) 1 and 3 only
(d) 1, 2 and 3
prelimsMocks.explanation: Dr. K. Kasturirangan was ISRO Chairman from 1994 to 2003, during which the PSLV program was operationalized, and initial work on Chandrayaan-1 began. While GSLV development also occurred, his tenure is more closely associated with PSLV's success and Chandrayaan-1's foundational work. He famously chaired the committee that drafted the NEP 2020. So, 2 and 3 are correct.

prelimsMocks.question: Consider the following statements regarding the National Green Hydrogen Mission:
options: 1. Green hydrogen is produced by separating hydrogen from water using renewable energy through a process called electrolysis.
2. The mission aims to make India a net importer of green hydrogen by 2030.
3. It is considered crucial for achieving India's target of net-zero emissions by 2070.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
(a) 1 only
(b) 1 and 3 only
(c) 2 and 3 only
(d) 1, 2 and 3
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. Green hydrogen is indeed produced via electrolysis using renewable energy. Statement 2 is incorrect; the mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and *export*, not an importer. Statement 3 is correct, as green hydrogen is a key component in India's strategy to achieve its net-zero emissions target by 2070.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India?
options: (a) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Board of Directors
(b) Ministry of Finance, Government of India
(c) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
(d) Indian Banks' Association (IBA)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a statutory body of the Reserve Bank of India, constituted by the Central Government, and is responsible for determining the policy interest rates required to achieve the inflation target.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Dekho Apna Desh' initiative, often seen in news, is associated with which of the following sectors?
options: (a) Defence Manufacturing
(b) Space Exploration
(c) Tourism
(d) Digital Literacy
prelimsMocks.explanation: 'Dekho Apna Desh' is an initiative launched by the Ministry of Tourism to encourage citizens to travel within the country and explore its diverse culture, heritage, and natural beauty.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the multifaceted impact of the recent India-Pakistan LoC skirmish on regional stability and India's internal security architecture. What diplomatic and strategic measures should India prioritize for de-escalation and long-term border management?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: India and its neighbourhood- relations; GS3: Security challenges and their management in border areas.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the LoC incident and its immediate implications. Discuss the impact on India-Pakistan relations, ceasefire agreements, and regional security dynamics. Analyze the internal security challenges for Jammu & Kashmir. Suggest diplomatic measures (dialogue, engagement through third parties, CBMs) and strategic measures (strengthening border infrastructure, intelligence, defensive posture, international outreach).

mainsMocks.question: Evaluate the significance of Dr. K. Kasturirangan's contributions to both India's space program and its education policy. How do such visionary leaders shape a nation's scientific and human resource development?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; GS2: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education, Human Resources.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin with Dr. Kasturirangan's dual legacy. Detail his role in ISRO (PSLV, Chandrayaan-1 foundations, self-reliance). Explain his leadership in drafting NEP 2020 (holistic education, skill development). Connect these contributions to nation-building, scientific temper, and human resource development. Conclude on the impact of visionary leadership on national progress.

mainsMocks.question: The National Green Hydrogen Mission is envisioned as a cornerstone of India's energy transition. Discuss the strategic imperatives driving this mission and the key challenges that need to be overcome for India to emerge as a global leader in green hydrogen production.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; Energy security.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its importance for India. Discuss strategic imperatives: energy security, decarbonization goals (net-zero by 2070), economic opportunities, reducing import dependence, global leadership. Outline key challenges: high production cost, scaling up renewable energy infrastructure, water availability, storage and transportation, developing robust supply chains, R&D investment. Conclude with policy recommendations and collaborative efforts needed.

quickBytes.text: The Line of Control (LoC) is a de facto boundary between India and Pakistan in Kashmir, established after the Simla Agreement of 1972.
Dr. K. Kasturirangan, former ISRO Chairman, also headed the committee that drafted India's National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the RBI is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water using electricity from renewable energy sources, a process called electrolysis.
ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches for future missions.
India and Japan share a 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership', emphasizing cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) like diabetes and hypertension are a growing public health challenge in India.
The PM-KISAN scheme provides income support of ₹6,000 per year to eligible farmer families through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).
Stupas are dome-shaped Buddhist structures, often containing relics, with Emperor Ashoka credited for their widespread construction.
The Digital India program aims to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.

GS1.headline: Ancient Buddhist Stupa Unearthed in Bihar, Sheds Light on Mauryan Era
GS1.explainer:
• Archaeologists have discovered a well-preserved ancient Buddhist stupa in the Vaishali district of Bihar, dating back to the Mauryan period.
• The excavation revealed intricate carvings, pottery fragments, and possibly relics, providing new insights into early Buddhist architecture and practices.
• This discovery is significant for understanding the spread of Buddhism during the reign of Emperor Ashoka and the cultural landscape of ancient Magadha.
• The site is expected to become a major archaeological and pilgrimage destination, boosting local tourism and research opportunities.
• Conservation efforts are underway to protect the newly found structure and surrounding artifacts.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Ancient Indian History
prelimsPoints: Stupas are dome-shaped structures used as a place of meditation or for housing relics of the Buddha or his disciples.
The Mauryan Empire (c. 322–185 BCE) was a geographically extensive Iron Age historical power in ancient India.
Emperor Ashoka is credited with promoting Buddhism and constructing numerous stupas and pillars.
Bihar is historically significant as the birthplace of Buddhism and Jainism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in re-evaluating our understanding of ancient Indian history and culture, with specific reference to Buddhist sites.
Analyze the contributions of the Mauryan Empire to Indian art, architecture, and the spread of religious philosophies.
Examine the challenges and opportunities associated with preserving and promoting archaeological heritage sites in India.
Evaluate the role of Bihar as a historical cradle of religious and cultural movements in ancient India.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists excavating an ancient stupa, revealing its dome structure and surrounding artifacts.
GS1.trivia: Vaishali is considered one of the earliest republics in the world and was a prominent center of Buddhism and Jainism.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Environmental Clearance for Critical Coastal Zone Project
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today upheld the environmental clearance granted to a major infrastructure project in a Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) area, dismissing petitions challenging its ecological impact.
• The ruling emphasized the need for a balanced approach between environmental protection and developmental imperatives, citing the project's strategic importance.
• The Court noted that all due processes under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, and CRZ Notifications were followed.
• Environmental activists expressed concern over the precedent set, while the government welcomed the decision as a boost for infrastructure development.
• The judgment highlighted the role of expert committees in assessing environmental impact and the judiciary's limited scope in technical matters.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
Environmental protection acts
prelimsPoints: CRZ notifications regulate activities in coastal areas to protect the marine environment.
The Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, is an umbrella legislation for environmental protection.
Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) is mandatory for certain projects to obtain environmental clearance.
National Green Tribunal (NGT) deals with environmental cases.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in balancing economic development and environmental conservation in India, with reference to the CRZ framework.
Analyze the role of the judiciary in environmental governance and the implications of judicial pronouncements on developmental projects.
Critically evaluate the effectiveness of the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process in ensuring sustainable development.
Examine the 'development vs. environment' debate in India and the need for robust regulatory mechanisms.
GS2.imageDescription: A coastal line showing a mix of natural environment and an ongoing infrastructure project.
GS2.trivia: The first Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) notification was issued in 1991 under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Summit Focuses on Indo-Pacific Cooperation and Economic Ties
GS2.explainer:
• Prime Minister of India and Japan's Premier held a bilateral summit in New Delhi, reaffirming their commitment to a Free and Open Indo-Pacific.
• Key discussions revolved around enhancing security cooperation, maritime domain awareness, and strengthening supply chain resilience in the region.
• Both leaders emphasized deepening economic partnership, particularly in areas of clean energy, digital technology, and infrastructure development.
• Agreements were signed to boost collaboration in defense manufacturing and facilitate Japanese investment in India's North-Eastern states.
• The summit underscored the growing strategic alignment between India and Japan in addressing regional and global challenges.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan share a 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership'.
Both countries are members of the Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) along with the USA and Australia.
Japan is a major investor in India's infrastructure projects, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.
The Indo-Pacific region is crucial for global trade and security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic convergence between India and Japan in the context of the Indo-Pacific region and its implications for regional security.
Discuss the multifaceted nature of India-Japan bilateral relations, covering economic, security, and cultural dimensions.
Evaluate the role of India and Japan in promoting a rules-based international order and countering challenges to regional stability.
Examine the potential for enhanced cooperation between India and Japan in critical and emerging technologies and defense manufacturing.
GS2.imageDescription: Prime Ministers of India and Japan shaking hands at a joint press conference.
GS2.trivia: Japan is one of the few countries with which India holds a '2+2' Ministerial Dialogue (Foreign and Defence Ministers).

GS2.headline: Union Health Ministry Rolls Out New Policy to Tackle Non-Communicable Diseases
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry launched a comprehensive new national policy aimed at strengthening prevention, diagnosis, and management of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs).
• The policy focuses on a multi-sectoral approach, integrating health promotion, early screening, and improved access to treatment for NCDs like diabetes, hypertension, and cancer.
• Key initiatives include expanding wellness centers under Ayushman Bharat and leveraging digital health platforms for patient management and data collection.
• Special emphasis is placed on lifestyle modifications, tobacco and alcohol control, and reducing unhealthy dietary practices.
• The move is crucial as NCDs account for a significant portion of morbidity and mortality in India, posing a major public health challenge.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
keyTerms: Ayushman Bharat Health and Wellness Centres
prelimsPoints: NCDs include cardiovascular diseases, cancers, chronic respiratory diseases, and diabetes.
Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY) provides health insurance cover.
Health and Wellness Centres (HWCs) under Ayushman Bharat aim to provide comprehensive primary healthcare.
India faces a dual burden of communicable and non-communicable diseases.
mainsPoints: Analyze the growing burden of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in India and its socio-economic implications.
Critically evaluate the key provisions of the new NCD policy and its potential effectiveness in addressing the challenge.
Discuss the role of public health infrastructure, digital health, and community participation in successful NCD prevention and management.
Examine how lifestyle factors and socio-economic determinants contribute to the prevalence of NCDs and what multi-sectoral interventions are needed.
GS2.imageDescription: A medical professional conducting a health check-up, symbolizing NCD prevention.
GS2.trivia: Globally, NCDs kill 41 million people each year, equivalent to 74% of all deaths.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Issues
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government policies and interventions for health sector

GS2.headline: Digital India Initiative Achieves New Milestones in Public Service Delivery
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) reported significant achievements under the Digital India program, particularly in enhancing public service delivery.
• New data shows a substantial increase in the number of digital transactions and online access to government services across various sectors.
• Initiatives like Aadhaar-enabled services, UPI payments, and the DigiLocker platform have streamlined processes and reduced corruption.
• The program has played a crucial role in improving governance, financial inclusion, and bridging the digital divide, especially in rural areas.
• Challenges related to digital literacy, cybersecurity, and infrastructure in remote regions continue to be addressed through ongoing policy interventions.
gsSubTopics: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
GS2.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: Digital India is a flagship program of the Government of India launched in 2015.
Its vision is to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.
Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identity number issued by UIDAI.
UPI is an instant real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative impact of the Digital India initiative on governance, public service delivery, and citizen engagement in India.
Analyze the successes and challenges faced by the Digital India program in bridging the digital divide and ensuring equitable access to digital services.
Evaluate the role of key digital platforms like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker in promoting financial inclusion and transparency.
Examine the future prospects and potential areas of improvement for India's digital governance ecosystem, considering cybersecurity and data privacy concerns.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting various digital services connecting citizens to government offices.
GS2.trivia: The 'JAM Trinity' (Jan Dhan-Aadhaar-Mobile) is a key enabler of the Digital India vision, facilitating direct benefit transfers.

GS2.headline: Smart Cities Mission Project Inaugurated in Pune, Focus on Sustainable Infrastructure
GS2.explainer:
• A significant project under the Smart Cities Mission was inaugurated in Pune, focusing on integrated command and control centers and sustainable urban mobility solutions.
• The initiative aims to enhance the city's liveability, promote efficient resource management, and improve public safety through technology.
• This project is part of Pune's larger Smart City plan, which emphasizes citizen participation and innovative urban governance models.
• Funding for the project comes from both central and state governments, along with private sector contributions.
• The successful implementation is expected to serve as a model for other cities participating in the Smart Cities Mission.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Development processes and the development industry- the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
prelimsPoints: Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015 to promote cities that provide core infrastructure, a clean and sustainable environment, and a decent quality of life.
The mission aims to drive economic growth and improve the quality of life of people by enabling local area development and harnessing technology.
Integrated Command and Control Centres (ICCCs) are a key component for smart city operations.
Pune is one of the 100 cities selected under the Smart Cities Mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and achievements of the Smart Cities Mission in India, citing examples of successful projects.
Analyze the challenges faced by urban local bodies in implementing Smart City projects, including funding, governance, and citizen engagement.
Evaluate the role of technology and innovation in transforming urban spaces and addressing issues like traffic congestion, waste management, and public safety.
Examine how the Smart Cities Mission contributes to India's broader sustainable development goals and urban planning strategies.
GS2.imageDescription: A modern cityscape of Pune with smart traffic signals and green spaces, symbolizing urban development.
GS2.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission covers 100 cities across India, selected through a competitive 'City Challenge' process.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Progress Reviewed by Ministry
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a comprehensive review of the implementation progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across states and Union Territories.
• The review highlighted advancements in curriculum reforms, vocational education integration, and digital learning initiatives.
• Challenges persist in teacher training, infrastructure development, and ensuring equitable access to quality education, particularly in remote areas.
• The NEP 2020 aims for holistic and multidisciplinary education, focusing on critical thinking and skill development from foundational to higher education levels.
• The Ministry reiterated its commitment to accelerating implementation, with a focus on collaborative efforts between central and state governments.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
It aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
Key features include a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure, vocational education integration, and emphasis on mother tongue as medium of instruction.
The policy seeks to increase the Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education to 50% by 2035.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the progress and challenges in the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in transforming India's education system.
Discuss how NEP 2020 addresses the skill gap and promotes holistic development among students, aligning with the demands of the 21st-century economy.
Evaluate the role of digital learning and technology in achieving the objectives of NEP 2020, especially in ensuring equitable access to quality education.
Examine the collaborative framework between central and state governments required for successful, large-scale educational reforms like NEP 2020.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting engaged in learning, symbolizing education reform.
GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century and aims to address the many growing developmental imperatives of India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Issues
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government policies and interventions for education sector

GS2.headline: UN Report Highlights Global Progress and Challenges in Achieving SDGs
GS2.explainer:
• A new United Nations report assessed global progress towards the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), noting mixed results five years before the 2030 deadline.
• While significant strides have been made in areas like poverty reduction and access to education, challenges persist in climate action, gender equality, and reducing inequalities.
• The report emphasized the disproportionate impact of global crises, such as pandemics and conflicts, on vulnerable populations and SDG achievement.
• It called for accelerated efforts, increased international cooperation, and innovative financing mechanisms to meet the ambitious targets.
• India's efforts in achieving several SDGs, particularly in clean energy and digital infrastructure, were acknowledged, though areas like hunger and health need further impetus.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are a collection of 17 interlinked global goals designed to be a 'blueprint to achieve a better and more sustainable future for all'.
They were set by the United Nations General Assembly in 2015 and are intended to be achieved by the year 2030.
NITI Aayog monitors India's progress on the SDGs.
The goals cover social, economic and environmental aspects.
mainsPoints: Analyze the global progress and persistent challenges in achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by 2030, in light of recent global crises.
Discuss India's contributions and specific challenges in meeting the SDG targets, especially in areas like poverty, hunger, and climate action.
Evaluate the role of international cooperation and multilateral institutions like the UN in fostering sustainable development across nations.
Examine the interconnectedness of the 17 SDGs and how progress in one area can influence others, emphasizing the need for integrated policy approaches.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the 17 Sustainable Development Goals icons, symbolizing global collaboration.
GS2.trivia: The SDGs succeeded the Millennium Development Goals (MDGs), which ran from 2000 to 2015.

GS2.headline: India and ASEAN Nations Discuss Deeper Trade & Investment Pact
GS2.explainer:
• India engaged in high-level discussions with the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) to explore opportunities for a deeper trade and investment agreement.
• The talks aimed at reviewing the existing ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA) agreement and identifying new sectors for enhanced cooperation.
• Focus areas included digital trade, supply chain resilience, green technologies, and greater market access for goods and services.
• Both sides expressed commitment to fostering economic integration and strengthening connectivity across the Indo-Pacific region.
• A potential upgrade to the AIFTA could significantly boost bilateral trade, which has seen steady growth over the past decade.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
India and its neighbourhood- relations
prelimsPoints: ASEAN is a regional intergovernmental organization comprising ten Southeast Asian countries.
India's 'Act East Policy' focuses on extended neighbourhood in Asia-Pacific.
AIFTA (ASEAN-India Free Trade Area) agreement came into force in 2010 for goods and 2015 for services and investment.
RCEP (Regional Comprehensive Economic Partnership) is a free trade agreement between the Asia-Pacific nations, which India chose not to join.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of India's economic engagement with ASEAN nations in the context of its 'Act East Policy' and the evolving Indo-Pacific dynamics.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges of upgrading the ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA) agreement for India's trade and manufacturing sectors.
Evaluate the role of regional trade agreements in fostering economic integration and strengthening supply chain resilience in the post-pandemic era.
Examine how India's bilateral and multilateral trade policies are shaping its position in the global economic architecture.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN nations arranged in a circle, symbolizing cooperation.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN was established on 8 August 1967, in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration).

GS3.headline: Tensions Flare Along LoC: India, Pakistan Exchange Fire in Border Skirmish
GS3.explainer:
• Indian and Pakistani soldiers briefly exchanged fire along the Line of Control (LoC) in the Poonch sector, marking a fresh escalation in border tensions.
• The incident involved small arms fire and light shelling, with no immediate reports of casualties on either side.
• Both nations have accused each other of initiating the firing, leading to diplomatic protests.
• The skirmish comes amidst ongoing efforts to maintain a fragile ceasefire agreement, raising concerns about regional stability.
• Security analysts suggest the incident might be a localized provocation or a response to perceived cross-border activities.
gsSubTopics: Border areas
India and its neighbourhood- relations
Security challenges and their management in border areas
prelimsPoints: Line of Control (LoC) is a de facto border between India and Pakistan in Jammu and Kashmir.
The 2003 ceasefire agreement between India and Pakistan aimed to reduce cross-border firing.
Poonch sector is a frequently active area along the LoC.
Indian Army and Pakistan Army are the primary forces deployed along the LoC.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of LoC skirmishes on India-Pakistan bilateral relations and regional stability.
Discuss the challenges in maintaining peace along the LoC and the effectiveness of existing confidence-building measures.
Examine India's border management strategy and the role of diplomatic channels in de-escalation.
Evaluate the internal security ramifications of increased border tensions for Jammu and Kashmir.
GS3.imageDescription: Indian Army soldiers patrolling a forward post along the Line of Control in a mountainous region.
GS3.trivia: The Line of Control (LoC) was established after the Simla Agreement of 1972, replacing the earlier ceasefire line.

GS3.headline: Veteran Space Scientist Dr. K. Kasturirangan Passes Away at 84
GS3.explainer:
• Dr. Krishnaswamy Kasturirangan, a towering figure in Indian space science and former Chairman of ISRO, passed away at the age of 84.
• He spearheaded India's space program during a critical phase, including the development of the PSLV and initial work on Chandrayaan-1.
• Dr. Kasturirangan also played a crucial role in shaping India's education policy, chairing the committee that drafted the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• His contributions extended to various scientific and governmental advisory roles, earning him numerous national and international accolades.
• The scientific community and national leadership mourned the loss of a visionary leader and institution builder.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Space technology
prelimsPoints: Dr. K. Kasturirangan was Chairman of ISRO from 1994 to 2003.
He oversaw the successful launch of the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV) and India's first moon mission, Chandrayaan-1.
He chaired the committee that drafted the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Awarded Padma Vibhushan, India's second-highest civilian honor.
mainsPoints: Discuss the impact of Dr. K. Kasturirangan's leadership on the growth and achievements of India's space program.
Analyze the intersection of science and policy, citing his contributions to both space technology and education reform.
Evaluate the role of visionary scientists in nation-building and fostering scientific temper in India.
Examine the legacy of India's space pioneers in achieving self-reliance in critical technologies.
GS3.imageDescription: A portrait of Dr. K. Kasturirangan, a distinguished scientist, smiling.
GS3.trivia: Dr. Kasturirangan's tenure as ISRO Chairman saw India launch its first operational remote sensing satellites and communication satellites using indigenous launchers.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science & Technology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Indian Space Programme and its pioneers

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Signals Cautious Stance Amid Global Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
• RBI Governor cited persistent global uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility, as key reasons for the cautious approach.
• The central bank also maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, indicating a continued focus on bringing inflation within target.
• Analysts anticipate that this stability will support economic growth while keeping inflationary pressures in check.
• The decision reflects a measured strategy to navigate complex domestic and international economic landscapes.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: The repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
The primary objective of the RBI's monetary policy is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target set by the government for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for inflation and economic growth.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the need to support economic recovery amidst global headwinds.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflationary pressures and demand-side imbalances.
Examine the coordination between fiscal and monetary policies in achieving macroeconomic stability in India.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) logo with a graph showing stable interest rates.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Functions of RBI and Monetary Policy Committee

GS3.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Shows Promising Progress in Pilot Projects
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced significant progress in the pilot projects under the National Green Hydrogen Mission, launched earlier this year.
• Several industrial clusters and research institutions have successfully demonstrated green hydrogen production and application in various sectors like refining and fertilizer.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Government incentives and public-private partnerships are driving innovation and investment in this nascent but crucial sector.
• Challenges remain in scaling up production, reducing costs, and developing a robust supply chain, but initial results are encouraging.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Energy security
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced through electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for India to become a global hub for green hydrogen.
It is considered a key component for achieving India's net-zero emissions target by 2070.
Applications include fuel for transportation, industrial feedstock, and power generation.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the potential of green hydrogen as a game-changer for India's energy security and climate change mitigation efforts.
Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission in achieving India's ambitious decarbonization targets.
Analyze the economic and technological challenges associated with scaling up green hydrogen production and its integration into the existing energy infrastructure.
Examine the role of government policies and international collaborations in fostering the growth of India's green hydrogen economy.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but it rarely exists in its pure form on Earth.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) for Future Missions
GS3.explainer:
