• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful test flight of its advanced Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) demonstrator.
• This critical test focused on validating autonomous landing capabilities and re-entry technologies, crucial for cost-effective space access.
• The RLV program aims to develop fully reusable orbital launch vehicles, significantly reducing the cost of satellite launches.
• This achievement marks a major step towards India's long-term goal of developing sustainable and competitive space transportation systems.
• Future phases will involve orbital flights and integration of propulsion systems for operational use.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Space technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) is a winged body aerospace vehicle.
The objective is to achieve low-cost access to space.
RLV technology is crucial for future human spaceflight missions like Gaganyaan.
It aims to make space missions more frequent and economical by reusing vehicle components.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's future space ambitions and its potential impact on the global space market.
Analyze how RLV development aligns with India's strategic goals of achieving self-reliance and commercial competitiveness in space technology.
Examine the technological challenges and breakthroughs required for developing fully reusable launch systems.
Evaluate the economic benefits and environmental advantages of reusable space launch vehicles compared to expendable ones.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) demonstrator landing on a runway.
GS3.trivia: The first RLV-TD mission, HEX (Hypersonic Flight Experiment), was successfully conducted by ISRO in 2016.

GS3.headline: Government Extends PM-KISAN Scheme Benefits to More Marginal Farmers
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved an expansion of the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) scheme, bringing more marginal and small farmers under its ambit.
• The revised guidelines aim to address existing inclusion-exclusion errors and ensure equitable distribution of financial assistance.
• PM-KISAN provides income support to farmer families to supplement their financial needs for agricultural inputs and domestic expenses.
• This move is expected to further boost rural incomes and alleviate financial distress among vulnerable farming communities.
• The government also announced measures to streamline the verification process and ensure timely disbursement of funds.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: PM-KISAN is a central sector scheme providing income support to all landholding farmer families.
Eligible farmer families receive ₹6,000 per year in three equal installments of ₹2,000.
The scheme is implemented through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) mode.
Exclusion criteria exist for certain categories of farmers, like institutional landholders and high-income taxpayers.
mainsPoints: Analyze the impact of income support schemes like PM-KISAN on farmer welfare and agricultural productivity in India.
Discuss the challenges in identifying eligible beneficiaries and ensuring effective implementation of direct benefit transfer schemes in agriculture.
Evaluate the role of government subsidies in addressing agrarian distress and promoting sustainable agricultural practices.
Examine the broader implications of such schemes for rural development and poverty reduction in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field with a digital payment symbol overlaid, representing PM-KISAN.
GS3.trivia: PM-KISAN was launched on 24 February 2019, with retrospective effect from 1 December 2018.

GS3.headline: India's Indigenous Defence Production Boosted with New Naval Destroyer Launch
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Navy officially launched its latest indigenously-built guided-missile destroyer, 'INS Vajra', at Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd (MDL).
• This launch marks a significant milestone in India's 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives for the defense sector.
• The destroyer is equipped with advanced stealth features, state-of-the-art weapon systems, and sensors, enhancing India's naval capabilities.
• The project has generated substantial employment and fostered technological self-reliance in warship building.
• 'INS Vajra' will undergo further sea trials before its formal commissioning into the fleet, bolstering India's maritime security.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: Project 15B is a class of stealth guided-missile destroyers being built for the Indian Navy.
Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd (MDL) is a premier shipyard in India.
'Make in India' in defence aims to reduce import dependence and boost domestic manufacturing.
Indian Navy is a crucial component of India's maritime security strategy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous defence production for India's strategic autonomy and national security, with special reference to naval shipbuilding.
Analyze the impact of 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives on the Indian defence manufacturing sector.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in developing advanced naval platforms and integrating cutting-edge technologies.
Examine the role of the Indian Navy in projecting power, ensuring maritime security, and protecting India's economic interests in the Indo-Pacific region.
GS3.imageDescription: A newly launched guided-missile destroyer, 'INS Vajra', at a shipyard, with naval officers observing.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the few countries with the capability to design and build advanced warships, including aircraft carriers and destroyers.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Indigenous defence production and Indian Navy

GS3.headline: Phase III of Namami Gange Programme Launched with Focus on Tributary Clean-up
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti launched the third phase of the Namami Gange Programme, shifting focus to the rejuvenation of the Ganga's major tributaries.
• This phase will emphasize decentralized wastewater management, afforestation along tributary banks, and community participation in river conservation.
• The previous phases primarily focused on the main stem of the Ganga, achieving significant progress in reducing pollution and developing infrastructure.
• The initiative aims to improve the ecological health of the entire Ganga basin, ensuring sustainable water resources and biodiversity.
• New technologies for real-time water quality monitoring and public awareness campaigns are integral to the expanded program.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
River interlinking
prelimsPoints: Namami Gange Programme is an integrated conservation mission approved by the Union Government in June 2014.
It aims at effective abatement of pollution, conservation, and rejuvenation of the National River Ganga.
The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) is the implementing agency.
Key tributaries of Ganga include Yamuna, Ghaghara, Gandak, Kosi, and Son.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and effectiveness of the Namami Gange Programme in achieving its objectives of Ganga rejuvenation, highlighting the shift in focus to tributaries.
Analyze the multi-sectoral approach required for river conservation, encompassing pollution control, ecological restoration, and community engagement.
Evaluate the challenges in managing the vast Ganga basin, considering industrial pollution, agricultural runoff, and urban wastewater discharge.
Examine the importance of public participation and technological interventions in ensuring the long-term sustainability of river cleaning initiatives.
GS3.imageDescription: A clean section of the Ganga river with green banks, symbolizing successful rejuvenation efforts.
GS3.trivia: The Ganga is India's longest river, flowing for over 2,500 kilometers from the Himalayas to the Bay of Bengal.

GS3.headline: India's Tourism Sector Records Robust Growth, Contributes to Job Creation
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism released data indicating a strong rebound and robust growth in India's tourism sector, surpassing pre-pandemic levels.
• International tourist arrivals and domestic travel have seen significant increases, driven by government promotional campaigns and improved infrastructure.
• The sector has emerged as a major contributor to job creation, particularly in hospitality, transport, and local artisan industries.
• Initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh' and promotion of niche tourism (adventure, eco, spiritual) have attracted diverse visitors.
• Challenges include sustainable tourism practices, skill development for tourism professionals, and enhancing global marketing efforts.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Tourism is the nodal agency for the development and promotion of tourism in India.
'Dekho Apna Desh' is an initiative to encourage citizens to travel within the country.
Tourism is a major foreign exchange earner for India.
India has a diverse range of tourist attractions, including cultural, historical, natural, and spiritual sites.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted contribution of the tourism sector to India's economy, including job creation, foreign exchange earnings, and infrastructure development.
Analyze the government's strategies and initiatives aimed at promoting tourism in India and attracting both domestic and international visitors.
Evaluate the challenges in developing a sustainable and inclusive tourism sector in India, considering environmental impact and local community benefits.
Examine the potential of niche tourism segments (e.g., medical, adventure, spiritual) in diversifying India's tourism offerings and attracting specific markets.
GS3.imageDescription: A vibrant collage of famous Indian tourist destinations, showcasing diversity.
GS3.trivia: The World Tourism Organization (UNWTO) defines tourism as 'travel for leisure, business, and other purposes'.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Growth and development of service sectors

sources: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
